F1.name: Emergency Parenting Time
F1.notes: Strong filing. Clear statutory right (MCL 722.27a). Well-supported by evidence.

F2.name: Fraud on Court
F2.notes: Strong evidence but tone must be measured. Avoid hyperbole. Stick to documented contradictions.

F3.name: Judicial Disqualification
F3.notes: Well-supported by violation data. Must be respectful and factual. MCR 2.003 is specific.

F4.name: §1983 Federal Complaint
F4.notes: Catz v Chalker supports viability. Damages must be itemized. Avoid overreach.

F5.name: MSC Superintending Control
F5.notes: HIGH sanctions risk. MSC grants these rarely. Must show exhaustion + extraordinary circumstances.

F6.name: JTC Complaint
F6.notes: No sanctions risk — JTC complaints are protected speech. File freely.

F7.name: Custody Modification
F7.notes: Strong basis under Vodvarka. Best interest factors favor Andrew (91 vs 57).

F8.name: COA Leave Application
F8.notes: Standard appellate practice. No sanctions risk for seeking leave.

F9.name: COA Appeal Brief
F9.notes: Already docketed (366810). Standard appeal. Focus on preserved errors.

F10.name: AGC Grievance
F10.notes: No sanctions risk — AGC complaints are protected. Barnes withdrawal supports filing.
